species: chronic liver injury can recruit bone marrow progenitors of LSECs that engraft and fail to fully differentiate, which creates an environment that is permissive for hepatic fibrosis.
tissue: the overall number of sprocs (CD133+ LSECs) in the liver increased from 1.5% to 9.4% in cirrhotic liver.
cell_type: Normal liver sinusoidal endothelial cells (LSECs) promote quiescence of hepatic stellate cells.
disease_state: chronic liver injury can recruit bone marrow progenitors of LSECs that engraft and fail to fully differentiate, which creates an environment that is permissive for hepatic fibrosis.
sample_source: Cirrhosis was induced with thioacetamide (TAA) in rats that had been transplanted with bone arrow from Lew-Tg(CAG-EGFP)ys rats.
cell_line: Breeding pairs of Lew-Tg(CAG-EGFP)ys Lewis rats were obtained from the National Institutes of Health.
sex: Lewis male rats were obtained from Harlan Corp.
strain: Breeding pairs of Lew-Tg(CAG-EGFP)ys Lewis rats were obtained from the National Institutes of Health.
